entry.title: Work Center Determination for Packing During Outbound Process
entry.content:
Recently a friend ask me for help, because he was not being able to dynamicly determine the correct work center on his POSC for the packing step. Although it is pretty easy, it is not clear how to do and due to that many times consultant determine fixed storage bin in the Process-Oriented Storage Control node on the POSC configuration.

The goal of this blog is to present how to make it, I will explore both options, the fixed and the dynamic methods.

For those who still have never setup the Process-Oriented Storage Control (aka POSC), let me add some context to what is POSC.

The POSC is a functionality designed to handle complex warehouse processes that involve multiple sequential steps upon the processes execution. POSC allows you to define and execute these steps in a structured manner, ensuring goods are processed efficiently and in the correct order.

The main activities perform with POSC are:

  • For inbound process, define a series of warehouse activities, such as unloading, deconsolidation, quality inspection, repackaging, and putaway. 
  • For Outbound process, define a series of warehouse activities, such as picking, repacking, staging and loading. 
  • The process is configured in alignment with your warehouse layout and business requirements. Each step corresponds to a physical or logical location in the warehouse.
  • Based on predefined rules, POSC determines the route goods will take through the warehouse, including interim stops like quality inspection or repackaging stations.
  • The system creates automatically the required warehouse tasks for each process step and guides workers through the execution.
  • It is particularly useful in warehouses dealing with goods requiring additional checks or processing before they can be stored or shipped from the warehouse.

How to determine the POSC

 

In order to determine the POSC, the system provides two options:

  • BY Warehouse Process Type (aka WPT), in the WPT you have two options:
    • Set a Warehouse Order Creation Rule (aka WOCR), which contain the POSC
    • Set the POSC directly in the WPT

  • BY Warehouse Order Creation Rule

Particularly, I never assign anything to the WPT, because the determination using WOCR has more dynamism. I am not exploring WOCR area in this blog, but there a plenty of blogs explaining in details how WOCR works and its determination, I read a few blogs and the ones below are which suggest to take a look:

  • SAP EWM Part 17: Warehouse Order Creation Rule written by Manish Gupta 
  • SAP-EWM- WOCR determination based on Activity area written by Rohela Khan

After read all the documentation above, you will be able to determine the POSC for your process.

Business Scenario

In my example, the dynamic determination was required because the warehouse has one physical place to perform the repacking during the outbound process, but for one process the consolidation group is relevant and for the other it is not.

Assumptions

For the configuration I am using there are below some assumptions:

  1. Product master data is maintained with a Stock Removal Control Indicator
  2. The picking is for products and not for HU (it would work, but this is my business case)
  3. Pick-HU is relevant for this POSC
  4. Storage bin in the pack work center is created

Configuration Steps

1 - Setup the Work Center

The only difference between this work center for the one which already exist in my system is the Check for Consolidation Group, in my scenario there is no check, except for the storage type and storage sections, all the other parameters are identical to the template work center created by the BC Set, just copy it and make your changes.

IMG Path: SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center > Define Work Center

Make sure to create a optimization for the work center determination, it will be important for the master data definition. As you can see, this is a sequential definition of which data has to be analyzed in order to determine the work center for the process in the most effectively sequence. 

IMG Path: SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center > Optimize Work Center Determination in Goods Issue

2 - Setup the Process-Oriented Storage Control

IMG Path:SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Warehouse Task > Define Process-Oriented Storage Control

Define the External Storage Process Step

In my example, I am using the standard External Step OB02 - Pack, in case it is used by another process, make a copy to assign to the Storage Process - Definition.

Define the Process-Oriented Storage Control

In this configuration you can define to use a fix storage type, storage section and storage bin for the packing work center, if you do not want to use as fixed, mark the checkbox Rules-Based. Also important to define which WPT you need to have the creation of WPT to move from pack station to the next destination.

Define the Storage Process

Define the name, description and direction of your storage process, in this example I am doing for outbound process, so my direction is Stock Removal. 

Define the storage process steps

For my business scenario, I only have three steps:

1 - Picking (OB01) - to create the task to remove the stock from its source

2 - Pack (OB02) - to perform the pack/repack of the product before stage

3 - Prepare for Loading/Staging in GI (OB03) - to carry the goods after repacked to the goods issue zone, this will nobe executed, as I am not creating the warehouse task.

In my example, the warehouse task for the step OB02 will be automatically created after you complete the picking to move to GI zone, this is determined by the checkbox  Automatic Creation of Subsequent Warehouse Task.

Once the configuration above is done, you have to assign the WOCR to your process as explained in the first part of this blog.

Master Data Definition

The steps are only required if you defined rule-based determination, if you setup fix storage type and storabe bin it is supposed to be already.

For the dynamic determination, there are two master data required, see below the transaction an the maintainenca.

Transaction: /SCWM/PACKSTDT - Work Center Determination in Goods Issue

In the first step of configuration, we need the determine the releance parameters during the determination, this is where it interfere in the process, in my case I am using only the activity area has parameter for the determination.

Transaction: /SCWM/TWORKST - Work Center: Define Master Data Attributes

Define the Storage Bin that must be determined for you work center  

Testing

Outbound Delivery 340676180

The final destination is the staging zone the handover area, this handover area is a specificity of the client, ignore this word, it is just a goods issue zone. 

Warehouse Order 

Look that the WOCR was determined, in my case for test purpose I assigned directly in the WPT and the storage process is defined in the WOCR. As I said, I prefer to use WOCR determination by Activity Area.

In the log of Wo creation, we can see how the system determine the destination bin, if confirms that it was due to storage control.

Confirming the picking warehouse task - step OB01 (Tcode - /SCWM/TO_CONF)

Note 1: I am confirming by the warehouse monitor, use the way you perfer.

Note 2: make sure to have a pick-HU assigned to the WO, otherwise the HU will not get warehouse order reference and the process step will no be assigned to HU.

Display WT for Outbound

After confirming the first WT, system does not generated a new WT, because the pack step is not complete.

Executing the repack (Tcode /SCWM/PACK)

In this example, I will pack the product, because I picking from the source a partial quantity and not the full HU. It can be done in the RF Enviroment or SAP GUI/Fiori. After completing the repack, the system will automatically generate the creation of the next warehouse task

Open the pack station for you warehouse request (outbound delivery), to display the stock moved to there.

You can see the pick-HU on the tree of the left side. To complete the step, mark the HU and click in the button Complete Process Step for HU.

Confirming the second warehouse task - step OB02

In my scenario, the WPT defined in the Process-Oriented Storage Control is set to confirm immediately, so the task was created and generated after I complete the step.

If I look at the warehouse tasks, I can see that my pick-HU is already moved to the handover area, which was originally my final destination.

Conclusion

What was presented in this blog is the methods you can apply in your system to determine tha pack station for the Outbound Process.

It could still have two steps, one for staging it to the door and another one to load the truck, the concept is the, although, different master data.

In my process it ends at this point, the loading is performed by 3PL (that's why my name is HANDOVER), once it is finished the PGI is posted automatically by TM integration.

For the ones who does not know, I hope it can help, any further comments or suggestion for blog, please put in the comments and I can try to provide it.

 

Kindly regrads, 

Janderson Livio

entry.title: Required Roles for SAP BW consultant to Work on SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

As a SAP BW consultant, I need to understand what roles or authorizations are typically granted to a BW consultant in SAP ECC to facilitate tasks such as:

• Extracting data using standard and custom extractors

• Accessing tables and data sources for creating data flows

• Running or debugging programs (if needed)

• Viewing configuration and function modules related to data extraction


It would be helpful if you could specify whether any specific SAP Basis roles, functional roles, or custom roles need to be requested.

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

Best regards,
Amir

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Packs: a Call to Action for SAP S/4HANA Customers
entry.content: Hi @VeeraRajachandrasekhar, you need to take into account that the commercial worlds of Business Suite and S/4HANA are completely separated. The end of use rights in S4 has no influence on support rules in the Business Suite world whatsoever, and vice versa. Other than that, I'm not in a position to comment on potential solution-specific issues.

entry.title: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

Could you someone help me? I have this error when I try to consume odata external in my cap project

Error: The server does not support the functionality required to fulfill the request

When I execute external entity request from my cap service returns this error.  

When I test the service locally it returns data

Best regards,

Angel Gutierrez

entry.title: Re: Required Roles for SAP BW consultant to Work on SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi Amir

In Support we request the authorizations, which are outlined in the KBA-s:

  • 3352612 Authorizations for support users in the BW-BCT and BC-BW* cases
  • 3351970 Authorizations for support users in the BW Warehouse Management Area

I this covers the majority of the actions that you listed.

Of course everything depends on how the authorization concept is done in the system and it might be needed to make further adjustments.

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: serial number material in a serial number finished go...
entry.content: Hello Adrien, did you find the solution to connect your raw material serial number with you finished good serial number ? In ECC there was an object called installed based where the connection was possible but I didn't find it in Public Cloud. And the following answer from SAP is not complete enough, serial number history is not suffisant.

entry.title: Re: Filing Status sync from BSI
entry.content: I think the information you are looking for can be found in SAP note 2968880 - Filing status 00 is not valid on date MM/DD/YYYY - HRPAYUS_MESSAGES205.

entry.title: Re: Power Designer Generation Origin property in repository
entry.content:
Hi Alexandre,

you have to find your PDM table in the PMOBJT repository table. It has column called HIST, which contains foreign key into PMTEXT table into TXID column. Join it and you get the value of the History property, which contains information about the generation link (from your CDM object into your PDM object). But it is not that simple, because it contains string like this:

ORG {967ED8D1-61C6-4CD7-AE22-57B97A48C8C2}
DAT 1732291988

So you have to understand the structure of this property and decompose it properly. Btw that 36 chars long text is ObjectID of the entity you are looking for.

I have years of experience in customizing and extending PowerDesigner so in case you need help in any PD-related area, you can find me on LinkedIn and I can help you quickly solve issues like this.

Regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: New Installation of SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 – Part 1 – Maintenance Planner
entry.content:
This procedure is Part 1 of the series which describes the very first steps to download the media. We will focus on creating Sandbox type instance with co-deployed (embedded) gateway.

Please find other blogs as follows at S/4HANA SQUARE ONE


                  SAP S/4HANA  SQUARE  ONE

GREENFIELD IMPLEMENTATION    PART1 - MPPART2 -  Installation
PART3 -  BP Activation
PART4 - Fiori ActivationPART5 - DATA MIGRATION
S/4HANA 2023fps1 fps0fps1 fps0fps1 fps0fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2022fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2021fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0 
S/4HANA 2020fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps2 fps1 fps0fps0

As a prep you may like to empty the download basket for your S-000 user so that any files added to the download basket are meant for this task and will help to ensure that only the required files are included.

As a prep you may like to empty the download basket for your S-000 user so that any files added to the download basket are meant for this task and will help to ensure that only the required files are included.

 


You need to login to SAP Maintenance Planner to access system assigned to your S-user. Please ensure landscape data is uploaded to SAP support portal via LMDB of SAP Solution Manager. The diagram below shows the Maintenance planner cloud service and how it integrates with Customer system.

 

 


The source system is first defined in Maintenance planner (1) generates (2) stack.xml, used for (3) Installation and (4) Upgrade and then system is (5) registered with Solution Manager. The system data is synchronized with Landscape Management Database (LMDB). From LMDB the data is (6) uploaded to customer profile in SAP support portal. (7) Data Fetch occurs using Solman daily job – “Landscape Fetch”.

If there is issue in replicating you may be asked to apply Note 2186164 – Problem in replicating systems to SAP Maintenance Planner

TIP: This data based on Customer Number is used by Maintenance Planner for all planning activities. Also ensure you use same Customer Number to define system in sapnet. Use the S-user id of this Customer Number.

Login to SAP Maintenance Planner and select the SAP Fiori tile Plan for SAP S/4HANA

In the next screen,choose Install a new SAP S/4HANA system, Manual Installation. Here you can click on the link for the SAP S/4HANA on SAP Help Portal for the documentation and click on Next.

In the next screen enter SID = S4H; select Target Version = SAP S/4HANA 2023; Target Stack = 02 (10/2024) FPS
TIP: The component SAP Predictive integrator is selected by default but please note this component will not be installed by SWPM/SUM but has to be manually installed on the database.
Click on Next.
TIP: The selection of component above will only download the files. You have to install them as per the procedure for the component. 

In the next screen choose Co-deployed with Backend. Please refer SAP Fiori Deployment Options and Landscape recommendations for detailed information. Please select checkmark Target Product Instances as applicable depending on which SAP FontEnd Applications you need among UI for SAP S/4HANA, SAP Human Capital Management, SAP Master Data Governance and SAP Travel Management.

In the next scree click on Continue Planning

In the next screen Click on Next

In the next screen select OS/DB dependent files checkmark Linux on x86_64 bit and click on Confirm Selection.

You will get warning as below. Go through the Note and click on Continue.

In the next screen click on Next.

Click on NON-ABAP and you find that only DE and EN are selected by default. Select any other languages you require for installation.

TIP: The files are selected above for download only. Later on during installation, you have to select the languages in the SUM tool.

In the next screen click on Download Stack XML, Download Stack Text File, Download PDF and Export to Excel to download the files to your desktop. Out of these you will have to ftp the stack.xml file to the server. It will look something like this – MP_Stack_1002146477_20241122_.xml

TIP: Before you click on Push to Download Basket on next screen, please ensure that download basket is empty so that the download contains only the files needed for SAP S/4HANA installation.

Click on Push to Download Basket. This will load the Download Basket with files you need.

In the pdf file downloaded above, in the Planned Changes section there is a link provided that takes you directly to Service Marketplace to download the media.

Download all the media and separate the following into separate directories for convenience under /sapmedia/download. This includes files from Maintenance Planner 

You need the files below for the installation, please check if these are included in the maintenance planner download:

SWPM20SP19_1-80003424.SAR – Latest version of Software Provisioning Manager
SUM20SP21_5-80002456.SAR - Latest version of Software Update Manager

SAP HANA DATABASE:
HANA Database and Database Client media is already provided in the Maintenance Planner download basket.

Please refer SAP Note 3426239 - SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Package Stack 02: Additional Release Information for SAP HANA database release details. However, please check Note to ensure there are no problems with the SAP HANA release you select. Refer SAP Note 2655761 - SAP S/4HANA - restrictions and recommendations regarding specific revisions of SAP HANA database for use in SAP S/4HANA

IMDB_SERVER20_080_0-80002031.SAR - Latest version of SAP HANA Database as of this blog (Linux x86_64 64bit)

IMDB_CLIENT20_022_32-80002082.SAR -  SAP HANA Client (Linux x86_64 64bit)

If you want to install LiveCache you will require the SAP HANA database LCAPPS plugin from Software Downloads => SUPPORT PACKAGES AND PATCHES => SAP HANA PLATFORM EDIT 1.0 =>  HANA LCAPPS => SAP HANA LCAPPS 1.00 

TIP: If the above path is not available, please search for below file in the url https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/softwarecenter/support/index 
IMDB_LCAPPS_2080_0-20010426.SAR - Corresponding version of SAP HANA LCAPPS (Linux x86_64 64bit)

Download all the media and separate the DB files IMDB* into separate directories for convenience under /sapmedia

OTHER FILES:
In addition, please download file/s for best practices content activation from SAP Note 34455517 - SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA 2023 Cloud, private edition FP-Stack 02 (10/2024) content activation note. The file will be in this format BPS4CONT202302_0_0-80008741.ZIP

crlbag.p7s from https://tcs.mysap.com/crl/crlbag.p7s

COMPONENT FOR INCIDENTS:

You can open OSS messages using component BC-UPG-MP

OTHER REFERENCES:

Maintenance Planner - User Guide
Maintenance Planner Help Portal
Maintenance Planner – Frequently Asked Questions

Mahesh Sardesai
Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group

entry.title: Parsing multipart/mixed-form data format in BTP CI
entry.content:
Dear Community,

BTP CI receives multipart/mixed-form data from OData V4 API, which requires parsing the message in case of an error. Error description appears in the multipart data response, technically  OData API call was successful,it says HTTP 200 ok.
Based upon data content received from OData, BTP CI derived whether a call has been processed successfully or not.

For that BTP CI is required to parse this message. and it is expected to read JSON structure of the error description and HTTP content text ( HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found) in the example of format,  how this will be achieved through BTP CI?

Example of Multipart/mixed data:

 --789901289D3B067293B4A5D8BC2033B80
        Content-Type: application/http
        Content-Length: 1279
        content-transfer-encoding: binary
        
        HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found
        Content-Type: application/json;odata.metadata=minimal;charset=utf-8
        Content-Length: 1097
        
        {
        "error": {
            "code": "/SCWM/ODATA_API/126",
            "message": "task item 1 doesn't exist in warehouse1.",
            "target": "$Parameter/_it",
            "@SAP__core.ContentID": "1",
            "details": {
                "message": "task 1 doesn't exist in warehouse."
            }
        }
    }

--789901289D3B067293B4A5D8BC2033B80--


Thank you in advance.

Yours sincerely,

Sateesh

entry.title: New Machine Learning features in SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08
entry.content:
With the SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 release, several new features have been released in the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL), an enhancement summary is available in the What’s new document for Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) enhancements overview with SAP HANA Platform 2.0 SPS 08.

The feature highlights for the current release are described in more detail below

Classification and regression enhancements

A new multi-task multilayer perceptron (MLP) function is introduced, enabling multi-label classification or multi-target regression scenarios. Using a multi-task learning neural network, a single ML model allows to predict multiple, related target columns at once, as the model captures both common features across tasks as well as task-specific information with the same prediction model.

  • It leverages the commonalities between related tasks to improve the performance, generalization, and training efficiency, and moreover enables efficient use of data, better feature extraction, knowledge transfer, regularization, and end-to-end learning.
  • Furthermore, the function supports early stopping using validation data to avoid overfitting.
  • Users of the new multi-task MLP also benefit from shorter MLP training times and models with improved accuracy.

     The new function provides unique prediction model capabilities for example scenarios like

  • automated multi-field value proposals or pre-filling of forms (e.g. Sales Order Automation)
  • or predicting multiple price-/sales-targets (average, minimum, etc.) in a single model.

  • For a more detailed introduction to the new algorithm see the following blog post Advancing to Multi-task Multilayer Perceptron: a new Neural Network design in HANA Machine Learning  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/advancing-to-multi-task-multilayer-perceptron-a-new-neural-network-design/ba-p/13730435

 

Unified regression and -classification now support permutation feature importance,

  • a new and trending method in global explainability to evaluate the contribution of individual features to the overall predictive power of a model.
  • This is achieved by measuring the decrease of a model’s performance when a feature‘s values are being shuffled around. A detailed explanation and examples are also given in this blog Global Explanation Capabilities in SAP HANA Machine Learning.

                  Classic feature importance vs permutation feature importance reports 

 

The unified regression function is enhanced with prediction- and confidence interval

  • Interval estimates and the point estimate of response values in the unified prediction or scoring regression procedure. The prediction interval predicts distribution of individual future observation of the target, while the confidence interval predicts the mean statistic of the future target population.
  • Now supported with Generalised Linear Models (GLM), Multiple Linear Regression (MLR), Random Decision Trees (RDT), and Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT).
  • Complementing this, a new interval quality function is added, which aids to evaluate the quality of an interval

 

A new outlier detection for regression function is added,

  • providing the ability to detect point outliers in data used when training linear or tree-based regression models using MLR and HGBT regressors.
  • Outliers in training data get identified based on residual analysis and outlier-score evaluation, then can be excluded for model training and allowing to build improved regression model predictions.

 

A new FairML classification and regression function is added

  • New function aiding to mitigate “bias” and potential “unfairness” in ML model predictions related to affected groups of individuals
  • Binary classification or regression models based on Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT) are supported
  • Fairness evaluation is aided with metrics like demographic parity, equalized odds, and more
  • For a detailed use case and function example see the following blog https://blogs.sap.com/2023/12/08/fairness-in-machine-learning-a-new-feature-in-sap-hana-pal/

 

The Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT) function has been improved

  • New objective function Huber Loss and reweighted square for more robust regression models
  • Improved classification models by support of F1, recall, precision as cross validation metrics and weight scaling of target values to address imbalanced classes or weight scale values related to different costs
  • For improved early stopping during model optimization, the validation metric for early stopping can now be explicitly set.

Further regression and classification enhancements included

  • Improved KNN search, by matrix-enabled search method
  • Option to limit results to top N classes in multi-class predictions with unified classification
  • Enhanced one-hot encoding of categorial features, supports aggregating infrequent values, into a single output for each feature (SVM, MLP, MLinR, MCLogR)
  • Data-parallel / massive execution support with Multiple Linear-, Exponential-, Bi-Variate Natural Logarithmic-, Bi-Variate Geometric-, Polynomial-Regression

 

Time series analysis and forecasting enhancements

New outlier detection function in time series analysis

  • Automatic or manual time series residual analysis, incl. intermittent time series detection, and missing value handling, auto smoothing of time series
  • Automatic selection of the outlier detection algorithm from the residual series incl. Z1, Z2, Inter-Quartile-Range, Mean absolute deviation, Isolation Forest and DBSCAN
  • Support of a voting ensemble across multiple outlier scoring metrics

 

Improved seasonality- and trend decomposition in time series analysis

  • Seasonal and trend decomposition using the STL-method (season, trend, low-pass window), based on LOESS (locally estimated scatterplot smoothing)
    and a super smoother function
  • Allowing for seasonality tests with more robust decomposition and less sensitive to outliers

Time series analysis permutation-based external feature importance evaluation

  • a new and trending method in global explainability to evaluate the contribution of individual features to the overall forecast model accuracy.
  • This is achieved by measuring the decrease of a model’s performance when a feature‘s values are being shuffled around. A detailed explanation and examples are also given in this blog Global Explanation Capabilities in SAP HANA Machine Learning.
  • Supported with the following time series algorithms ArimaX, Bayesian Structural Time Series (BSTS), Long term time series (LTSF), Additive model time series analysis (AMTSA)

Forecast prediction interval evaluation

  • a new interval quality function is added, which aids to evaluate the quality the forecasted prediction interval

Long term time series (LTSF) enhancements

  • New Neural Network model types (NLinear, DLinear, XLinear) for improved speed and forecast accuracy
  • Rigorous decomposition support of neural network time series models into TREND, SEASONAL and EXOGENOUS_CONTRIBUTIONS parts

 

New data-parallel, massive execution time series function support

  • Online BCPD, Forecast Accuracy Measure, Time series outlier detection, Single Exponential Smoothing, Double Exponential Smoothing, Triple Exponential Smoothing, Brown Exponential Smoothing, Croston's Method, Linear Regression with Damped Trend and Seasonal Adjust, White Noise Test, Seasonality Test, Trend Test, Change-Point Detection, Intermittent Time Series Forecast (ITSF)
  • Allowing segmented time series analysis and forecasting and fastest result when modelling thousands of time series in parallel, identified by a segmentation,  grouping column

 

 

AutoML and ML pipelines enhancements

General AutoML configuration and optimization improvements

  • Optimized pipeline operator selection, considering operator sequence and importance using operator connection constraints
  • Finetuning phase of best pipeline after initial AutoML optimization, by further evaluation the remaining parameter combination options and without changing the selected pipeline operators itself
  • Support of random search optimization for small AutoML configurations and faster results, for example with simple time series scenarios
  • Faster AutoML pipeline predictions interpretability based on a more light-weight shapley explainer model (SHAPGlobal surrogate). More details can be found in the following blog post Demystifying Pipeline Explanation for Time Series Data.
  • AutoML scenario configuration verification option
  • Pipeline predictions supporting more algorithm specific parameters
  • Score function for hold-out sample based classification / regression model evaluation
  • Improved AutoML progress logging in Python ML client, with full log information

AutoML Time Series enhancements

  • Support for auto exponential smoothing, polynomial feature generation,
    HGBT-/ MLinR- regression operators for time series
  • SPEC as a new evaluation metric supported

New data-parallel AutoML and ML Pipeline modeling functions allow for developing and executing multiple models in parallel by

  • building AutoML classification, regression or time series models in parallel on data subsets, identified by a grouping column,
  • using the new massive AutoML and Pipeline SQL procedures

This allows for fastest parallel AutoML modeling, like e.g. segmented AutoML time series. At the same time SAP HANA Cloud’s database workload management is best applied to control and limit compute resource consumed.

 

 

Further AI function enhancements

New Quantile Transform data preprocessing

  • The Quantile Transform automatically applies a non-linear transform, that maps numeric input columns of varying data distributions, into a uniform or normal target distribution making it more suitable as an input for many ML models.
  • Preserves the rank order of the data, and reduces the impact of outliers, makes data less sensitive to extreme values

New financial data analysis functions

  • Benford Analysis, trending algorithm used to detect anomalies in financial data
    • One of the (not so) well-known statistical observations is the fact that in many datasets the leading significant digits are not equally distributed. If all digits were represented equally, then they would appear 11.1 percent (1/9TH) of the time. However, when analyzing real-world datasets, it is revealed that the distribution of the leading digits follows the Bedford’s law, also known as the first-digit law.
    • It is now very easy to validate if a dataset obeys Bedford’s law or not. A first step means, very commonly applied in financial applications is to detect unexpected value distributions and e.g. potential fraudulent transaction data.
  • Hull-White Model, trending used in modeling the time evolution of interest rates, rates, which are required for price estimation of financial instruments based on interest rate derivatives.
    • To apply the Hull-White model it first needs to be adopted to match existing market conditions (interest rates). This is achieved  by providing the values of the drift term of the Hull-White model as a time series as input table. The simulation will then provide the mean value for a given number of simulation paths (also specified as an input parameter), their variance, as well as the upper and lower bounds.

New MLP Recommender function

  • Powerful neural network function to predict binary targets such as click-through rates, a fundamental part in recommender systems.
  • The MLP recommender function does not require specific user and item details, and can include extra data features, unrelated features without any user connection.
  • The algorithm employs a dual-stream MLP framework, capable of handling larger-scale and “complicated” input data, designed to boost the recommendation performance.

Text processing enhancements

  • New RDT model-based text classification
  • Improved TF-IDF providing control over stopword usage

Improved Clustering

  • Pre-calculated distance matrices supported with agglomerate hierarchical- and spectral clustering with the unified clustering function
  • Improved one-hot encoding support with spectral clustering

Data-parallel / massive execution support

  • Isolation Forest, Variance Test, Inter-Quartile Range, Fast Fourier Transform, K-Optimal Rule Discovery (KORD), K-Means, FP-Growth

 

Automated Predictive Library

Automated Predictive Library (APL) 2425 release enhancements

  • Reduced execution time around 15% for binary classification or regression models (gradient boosting), and around 35% for multi-class classification.
  • Better estimates and faster calculation of variable interactions
     See what's New 2311
  • Local explanations for time series See what's New 2403

 

Python machine learning client enhancements

The full list of new methods and enhancements within the hana_ml releases 2.17-2.22 are summarized in the changelog for hana-ml as part of the documentation.

Selected key enhancements include

AutoML and pipeline modeling improvements

  • Visual editor support for the AutoML scenario configuration

 

  • Massive, data parallel AutoML support 

  • PipelineProgressMonitor python runtime_platform specification for jupyter, vscode, bas, console
  • AutoML optimization enhancement with connection constraints and connection visualizations

     

More detailed references to the described enhancements can also be found in the New Machine Learning features in SAP HANA Cloud - SAP Community blog posts.

Furthermore you can find example notebooks illustrating the highlighted feature enhancements under hana-ml-samples/Python-API/pal/notebooks at main · SAP-samples/hana-ml-samples · GitHub, like for example H2SPS08_AutoML.ipynb, H2SPS08_RegressionClassification.ipynb, H2SPS08_TimeSeries.ipynb, H2SPS08_MiscEnhancements.ipynb and H2SPS08_Design-time code generation.ipynb.

Explore and enjoy all the new capabilities!

entry.title: Difference in Ext. Amount LC (EXBWR) & Amt.in Loc.Cur. (DMBTR)
entry.content:
Hi All

I have a material with moving average price as 2 USD per piece. There is no PO & initial stock was uploaded at the same price. 1 CS=144 pieces. The moving average price is 2 USD.

If I create Goods Issue with movement type 201 for 7 pieces. I entered amount in field EXBWR manually as 100. After posting both Ext. Amount LC (EXBWR) & Amt.in Loc.Cur. (DMBTR) are same i.e. 100. The system just gave warning message of M7 306.

If I create Goods Issue with movement type 201 for 7 pieces. I entered amount in field EXBWR manually as 9999. After posting Ext. Amount LC (EXBWR) & Amt.in Loc.Cur. (DMBTR) are different & the difference got posted to PRD account. I understand the difference is creating & subsequently posting only when it is exceeding 100% of moving average price. Where is this setting which tells system to post to PRD account when it is exceeding 100% of moving average price? I see tolerance key VP is set at 10% & it starts giving warning message once EXBWR exceeds 10% of moving average price. However I did not find a setting which mentions that it should go to PRD account once EXBWR crosses 100% variance as compared to moving average price.

Regards

Pankaj

entry.title: Incorrect Learning Assign Date stored in SuccessFactors Learning - Time Zone management
entry.content:
Hi,

since the 2H2024 release, the learning assign date recording in the LMS data base has changed. I've seen no communication about that (please let me know if I missed one), but a KBA has been released, which I discovered when the support closed my ticket as the issue is considered as an expected behaviour of the system.

Faced situation:

When a learning item is assigned to a user in SAP SuccessFactors Learning, the assign date stored and displayed is the day before the actual assignment date. I.e. if you assign an item to a user today, the assign date mentioned in the LMS is yesterday.

Impacts:

  • Wrong user experience
  • Inaccurate information stored/displayed/reported on => potential issue in compliance and legal reporting
  • Inaccurate training due date calculation => potential issue in compliance and legal reporting
  • Impossible to reassign a course on the same day of its completion. 
    Indeed, if you assign an item today at 15:00 after the user has completed it at 14:00, as the assign date is recorded as yesterday, today's completion is considered to have applied to (i.e. completed) yesterday's assignment, so the assignment does not appear in the user's learning plan.
    => Issue when you test an online item you are building, when you do demos during an LMS training, and of course in real life use cases when you need to reassign a training to comply to specific retraining requirements.

Explanation:

3490433 - How is Assignment Date Stored in LMS Database
With 2H2024 release, the Learning Engineering Team changed the way the assignment date is stored in the LMS database: 
The assignment date selected by the admin will be stored as 00:00:00, using the admin's current time zone, and then converted to UTC.
Example, with an admin connected in the CET time zone which is 1h ahead of UTC:

  • The assignment is done at 15:10 CET on 22 November.
  • The system makes it 00:00 CET and then converts it to UTC.
  • The final stored date is 23:00 CET on 21 November 

Solution:

There is currently no solution proposed, only a suggestion to raise an enhancement request.

My demand:
The solution should be to simply store the accurate date and time of assignment in the data base. This should not be problematic in any case.
Before escalating, I would like to know if any of you has already escalated this issue, in order to regroup and give more weight to that escalation.
Do you see additional impacts than the ones above?

Thanks a lot in advance!

Pauline

SAP SuccessFactors Learning @Marine

entry.title: Field-Symbol Behavior in BW Virtual Characteristics/Key Figures BADI
entry.content:
#SAPBW #RSR_OLAP_BADI

In the IF_EX_RSR_OLAP_BADI~COMPUTE section, I am assigning a Key Figure to a field symbol (type ANY).

ASSIGN COMPONENT P_KYF_ZVK_STVAL OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_ZVK_STVAL>.

At times, this field symbol is a type P (value field) and other times it becomes a TYPE u (a structure). When it is a structure, the program dumps to ST22.   Does anyone know why this is occurring?  Any help would be greatly appreciated.

Here is the code for the COMPUTE section:

  method IF_EX_RSR_OLAP_BADI~COMPUTE.

  DATA:
*        L_QTY        TYPE I,
        L_QTY        TYPE /BI0/OITOTALSTCK,
        L_UNIT_PRICE TYPE /BI0/OINETPRICE,
        L_EXT_VALUE  TYPE /BIC/OIZVK_STVAL,
        L_COUNTRY(2),
        MATERIAL     TYPE /BI0/OIMATERIAL,
        PLANT        TYPE /BI0/OIPLANT,
        BATCH        TYPE /BI0/OIBATCH,
        WBS          TYPE /BI0/OIWBS_ELEMT,
        COUNTRY      TYPE /BI0/OICOUNTRY,
        VENDOR       TYPE /BI0/OIVENDOR,
        TOT_STK      TYPE /BI0/OITOTALSTCK,
        PRICE        TYPE /BIC/OIZVK_STVAL,
        L_TYPE(1).

  FIELD-SYMBOLS:
* Characteristics
        <FS_MATERIAL>  TYPE ANY,
        <FS_PLANT>     TYPE ANY,
        <FS_BATCH>     TYPE ANY,
        <FS_WBS>       TYPE ANY,
        <FS_MAT_TYPE>  TYPE ANY,
        <FS_COUNTRY>   TYPE ANY,
        <FS_VENDOR>    TYPE ANY,
*   Key Figures
        <FS_ZVK_STVAL> TYPE  ANY.


  IF I_S_RKB1D-COMPID IS NOT INITIAL AND
       I_S_RKB1D-COMPID = 'ZQ_TD_0IC_MP04_Q002'.


  ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0MATERIAL  OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_MATERIAL>.
  IF P_CHA_0PLANT IS NOT INITIAL.
     ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0PLANT  OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_PLANT>.
  ENDIF.
  IF P_CHA_0BATCH IS NOT INITIAL.
     ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0BATCH     OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_BATCH>.
  ENDIF.
  IF P_CHA_0WBS_ELEMT IS NOT INITIAL.
     ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0WBS_ELEMT OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_WBS>.
  ENDIF.
  IF P_CHA_0MATERIAL__0MATL_TYPE IS NOT INITIAL.
     ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0MATERIAL__0MATL_TYPE  OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_MAT_TYPE>.
  ENDIF.
  IF P_CHA_0MATERIAL__0MATL_TYPE IS NOT INITIAL.
     ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0PLANT__0COUNTRY  OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_COUNTRY>.
  ENDIF.
  IF P_CHA_0VENDOR IS NOT INITIAL.
     ASSIGN COMPONENT P_CHA_0VENDOR    OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_VENDOR>.
  ENDIF.
  UNASSIGN <FS_ZVK_STVAL>.
  ASSIGN COMPONENT P_KYF_ZVK_STVAL  OF STRUCTURE C_S_DATA TO <FS_ZVK_STVAL>.

  IF <FS_MAT_TYPE> EQ 'UNBW'.
* Calculate <FS_ZVK_STVAL> = <FS_TOTALSTCK> * L_UNIT_PRICE.
     CLEAR: L_UNIT_PRICE, TOT_STK, VENDOR, WBS, BATCH, PLANT, MATERIAL, L_COUNTRY, L_QTY, L_EXT_VALUE.
      L_COUNTRY = <FS_COUNTRY>.
      MATERIAL  = <FS_MATERIAL>.
      PLANT     = <FS_PLANT>.
      BATCH     = <FS_BATCH>.
      WBS       = <FS_WBS>.
      VENDOR    = <FS_VENDOR>.
*      MOVE <FS_TOTALSTCK> TO L_QTY.
*      TOT_STK     = <FS_TOTALSTCK>.

     CALL FUNCTION 'ZBW_GET_UNVALUATED_STK_PRICES'
       EXPORTING
         I_MATERIAL         = MATERIAL
         I_PLANT            = PLANT
         I_BATCH            = BATCH
         I_WBS              = WBS
         I_COUNTRY          = L_COUNTRY
         I_VENDOR           = VENDOR
      IMPORTING
        E_UNIT_PRICE       = L_UNIT_PRICE
*        E_CURRENCY         =
               .

     MOVE L_UNIT_PRICE TO L_EXT_VALUE.
     <FS_ZVK_STVAL> =  L_EXT_VALUE.

  ENDIF.
ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2410 Now Available – What’s New?
entry.content:
The latest update of SAP Service and Asset Manager, version 2410, includes powerful new features designed to support all our users, from maintenance and safety technicians to field service pros. Key updates include online work order search, configurable workflows, and specialized support for service quotations and purchase requisition. User experience improvements and robust backend support make service execution seamless across industries.

Please read on to learn more.

Key features delivered in the 2410 release

Configurable workflow for service execution

Every customer has unique needs and protocols when it comes to asset management and maintenance. Whether they are following strict government regulations or managing their customers’ expectations of quick and efficient service, the steps involved in maintenance execution must be followed exactly. With this release, we lay the foundation for no-code customizable workflows that may be tailored to our customers’ specialized workflows.

Mobile statuses, e.g., Ready, In Progress, Complete, are coded into SAP Service and Asset Manager. These may be changed to suit a custom workflow; however, backend and mobile client customizations are required. Providing a no-code option lets customers define their own mobile flows without changing anything in the SAP backend or the SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile application.

Using the Mobile Application Integration Framework (MAIF), administrators can map mobile statuses to a custom workflow. Steps can be named as needed, such as Travel, Work, Finish, and additional steps may be introduced to the flow. The technician will see these steps as a progress indicator on the order screen.

Online search for orders and notifications

Expanding on the online search capabilities from the last release, we add an online search for work orders and notifications. Online search is available on the home page, in the side menu, and from the work order and notification list pages.

A technician may search by criteria. A single object can be searched by inputting or scanning the object ID, or retrieve a larger set searching by description, category, plant details, location, work center, and/or status. A list of matching objects is presented to the technician who then may download objects to the device.

On the business side, the number of technical objects that are downloaded in a sync can be limited only to those most relevant to the technician’s work, thus improving sync times. Additional objects then may be downloaded as needed.

Support for service quotations and purchase requisitions

During service calls and inspections, technicians often find additional or follow-on maintenance needs. A new feature available for the professional Field Service Technician persona brings service quotation to the mobile application.

The technician may generate a service quotation directly from the field, including maintenance details and labor estimates, with specific service, part, and expense items listed. Once the data is synced, the backend system will execute the next steps for the customer.

Service quotations are currently only available for S/4HANA Service customers.

Equally common, a field service technician working on-site needs parts that are not immediately available. Creating a purchase requisition in the field is good for efficiency and accuracy.

The technician may request parts are ordered from a supplier or another warehouse on with the mobile application. The PR document is created with the requested materials listed, and open purchase requisitions may be viewed for tracking purposes.

Purchase requisitions are currently only available for SAP ECC Customer Service customers.

Enable safety technician workflows for maintenance technician

Safety technicians are generally maintenance technicians with specialized training or certification. SAP Service and Asset Manager includes a safety technician persona with a user interface specifically designed for efficiently executing a high volume of isolation tasks. When safety and maintenance tasks are assigned to the same technician, the 2410 release brings full safety technician functionality to the professional maintenance technician persona.

When viewing a work order, maintenance technicians can view the work permits and approvals associated with the order. They can also view certificates and execute lock-out/tag-out operational items. When enabled, safety-related navigation will show in the side menu for easy access to relevant list pages.

This release also introduces support for additional isolation item options to create ad-hoc/temporary isolation points that lack master data.

Additional features

User experience improvements

The SAP Service and Asset Manager user experience sees several significant updates in this release related to critical feedback from customers and partners.

Updated toolbar

The SAP Fiori mobile design introduces a new FioriToolbar that has now been implemented in the mobile application. The toolbar brings a consistent button layout across the application, as well as semantic coloring to indicate primary, secondary, or negative actions with corresponding colors.

Detail page enhancements

The team continues to update detail pages across the application, and this release enhances the notification and suboperation detail pages with object icons and layouts that prioritizes the most relevant content.

Supports images in hierarchy and BOM views

When viewing objects in the hierarchy and bill of materials (BOM) views, icons denote the type of object for easier identification. If the assets have photos attached in the master data records, those will show in place of the icons.

Additional options for quick action buttons

Quick action buttons now have additional non-default options. Users can set their own preferences to show the buttons most useful to them.

Handle multiple attachment selection in attachment form cell

When attaching photos and files across the application, users may now select more than one.

Field service technician features

Field service technicians can streamline their tasks with features that support efficient asset and service management. From creating asset to capturing detailed notes, and adding business partners, these tools enhance productivity and precision during on-site operations.

Table view for items

For customers using SAP S/4HANA Service, service order items are now displayed in sortable tables on the service order detail page and service order items list page, where edits can be completed directly on the table.

Support for adding and editing equipment and functional location

A field service technician may install new equipment or components at a customer site or set up temporary equipment crucial for on-site tasks. The field technician can use the mobile application to create equipment and functional locations so they can be tracked properly in the SAP backend.

Support for different note types

This release supports different note types available in SAP S/4HANA Service. Users may choose the note type and then input the text. Note types such as Internal Note, Problem Description, etc. are saved as their own long text.

Support additional reference objects

As a field service technician, the reference objects are crucial to understand and efficiently execute the tasks required. The service order detail page links to detailed information about the products and objects involved in the service execution.

Add business partners

Having up-to-date information about the facilities managers, site supervisors, or safety officers at the customer's site is essential. While on site, the field service technician can add new business partners when she learns about personnel changes at the customer site.

Currently only available for SAP S/4HANA Service customers.

Dynamic Forms for S/4HANA Service

SAP Dynamic Forms is now supported for both SAP S/4HANA Service and Customer Service customers.

Maintenance technician features

Enhancements for the defense and security industry improve maintenance management and productivity. Features include emergency work requests and standard text templates for quickly populating long text fields, enabling more efficient operations.

Defense and security industry support

In the backend, maintenance planning for defense customers involves specific objects and codes used for organizing, tracking, and scheduling maintenance tasks.

Pieces of defense equipment that require maintenance may move frequently, so up-to-date tracking is critical for both the customer and the technician.

Support phase-model notifications for emergency work requests

For customers using phase-model, technicians can now create a maintenance request as emergency work. This designation lets the request follow an expedited path to execution.

Support standard text templates for operations

Text templates configured in the backend system are now available on the mobile application. The technician can select from a list of templates when creating or updating an operation to automatically populate the long text fields.

This is particularly useful when certain tasks need to follow a standard format or include specific instructions, guidelines, or safety measures.

Backend features

Support for CDS Views

CDS (Core Data Services) views are standardized and reusable data models that can be leveraged across various SAP applications. With support in this release, customers may generate a report of mobile transaction history for a set of business object types, mobile users, or mobile applications.

Data points from the mobile application can be used to build intelligent operational dashboards. For example, understanding the meantime to repair and meantime to complete the work can provide valuable insights into technician productivity.

For customers using SAP Analytics Cloud, the SAP Maintenance Assistant CDS view is available as a data source. Learn more about integration with SAP Analytics Cloud using MAIF data sources.

Scanning using third-party scanner integrations

Many customers choose Samsung Knox and Zebra DataWedge to enhance Android device security, improve data capture, and increase operational efficiency in inventory and warehouse management.

The mobile application now supports scanning from both devices. The scanner can be enabled in the Profile Settings to create a seamless experience with SAP Service and Asset Manager.

Configuration details

Features described above may be optional and not be default-enabled, and integrations with other SAP products may require additional licensing.

Please refer to the updated blog post Configuring Features and Personas in SAP Service and Asset Manager to learn about assigning personas and supported features for each persona.

Release available now

The application is publicly available and includes an offline demo mode for viewing and testing the extensive functionality with maintenance technician, field service technician, safety technician, and inventory clerk personas included.

The application metadata is now available for licensed customers and partners on the SAP Support Portal Software Center and the mobile client may be downloaded from the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.

Share your ideas

We encourage you to learn more about submitting improvement requests to influence future releases of SAP Service and Asset Manager. Your ideas are the driving force behind our innovation.

Explore other SAP mobile applications

SAP has a powerful suite of SAP native mobile applications. Follow the MXE Community for latest updates.

entry.title: Re: Has anyone noticed new hires disappearing after manag...
entry.content:
Hi,

If the hire is future dated, still the status is 'inactive'. Search any active employee and there is a small arrow button next to in the quick card, click on it and then check mark 'include inactive employee in search' checkbox. You will be able to see the upcoming hires.

Also, you can go to onboarding dashboard and search for that new hire. click on the action button on the right hand top, click on the new hire data, it will take you to the upcoming hire's profile.

Make sure you have required permissions in EC.

Best,

Chandra

entry.title: Build a dynamic rolling forecast with SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Excel
entry.content:
This blog guides users on creating asymmetric table structures with the help of the newly introduced SAP.BLOCK and SAP.ASYMMETRICFILTER formulas. These formulas significantly improve table filtering, allowing users to apply asymmetric filters, e.g. to retrieve actual data for the previous half-year while simultaneously displaying forecast data for upcoming months.

Previously, users could leverage the SAP.GETDATA formula to access specific data points and construct asymmetric layouts, as outlined in @bigonnet blog post. However, now it's feasible to perform these actions directly on the table, unlocking the potential for table operations such as data entry, and concurrently diminishing the number of required backend calls.

In the example, accounts are listed vertically in column B. The horizontal axis displays an asymmetric time-version structure (actual data for Jun 24-Oct 24 and forecast data for Nov 24-Mar 25) in row 18-19. This structure is flexible and can be customized by the user within the Excel sheet (cells B7-B12). The resulting asymmetric "Version+Time" filter, a product of the formula, is visible in the designer panel of the table.

Asymmetric table layout with SAP.ASYMMETRICFILTER

To begin, a table (Table1) in SAP Analytics Cloud for Microsoft Excel needs to be created. This table should have the account dimension as rows and the version and time dimensions as columns.

The selection is designed with simple Microsoft Excel drop-down menus which allow the user to specify the month, year and the number of months to look ahead/back. While more sophisticated solutions may exist, this approach is sufficient for explaining the formula. To simplify the explanation, we'll assume the same timeframe for both past and future periods.

Selection for time within Excel

Given a selected month, such as October 2024 (month 10), Excel standard formulas like VLOOKUP and INDEX are used to determine the relevant time intervals. Five months are chosen to look both backward and forward in time.

For the actual data:

  • Start month: June 2024 (month 06)
  • End month: October 2024 (month 10)

For the forecast data:

  • Start month: November 2024 (month 11)
  • End month: March 2025 (month 03 of the following year)

Helper table

This helper table supports the SAP.BLOCK formula, which creates blocks for use in SAP.ASYMMETRICFILTER. Our example uses two SAP.BLOCK formulas: one for the "Actual" version (June to October 2024) and another for the "Forecast" version (November 2024 to March 2025).

=SAP.BLOCK(P57,Q57,P58,Q58#)

These two blocks are then combined and used within the SAP.ASYMMETRICFILTER formula to generate the filter criteria for the table (Table1). This formula can also accommodate multiple tables at once to define complex filtering conditions.

=SAP.ASYMMETRICFILTER("Table1",P68#,S68#)

The asymmetric filter, along with other filters, is displayed in the table's designer panel. The following image displays the month transition to November (11) with a 6-month look-ahead/back period:

 

SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft Excel will undergo continuous feature development in 2025. All details you find on the SAP roadmap explorer.

@alex_bundschuh

I noticed that there exists a restriction in the Partner Directory that you are unable to create duplicate pid for the same agency, but different ids. That is the below example will NOT work: 

{ "Agency": "ABC",  "Scheme": "SenderInterface",  "Id": "identification_111",  "Pid": "ALT_PID"}
{ "Agency": "ABC",  "Scheme": "SenderInterface",  "Id": "identification_222",  "Pid": "ALT_PID"}
This example works only when the Agency is different.  However, in reality we do have numerious situation where we would like intentionaly to make multiple messages from the same sender to be handled in the same way. For example,  a numerious iDocs can be simply transferred from one system to another system through CPI without mapping or converter required.  Without creating a specific iflow, such a situation can not be directly handled.  My solution for now is that instead of using SAP_Sender as agency, we can use SAP_Sender+MessageType as agency to achive such a goal. 
 
Is there a plan in future release to handle such a situation?

entry.title: How to mass create batch for process orders?
entry.content:
I would like to mass-create a batch for multiple process orders. Is there any option?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass create batch for process orders?
entry.content:
Hi,

I understand you're looking for a way to mass-create batches for process orders. Currently, there isn't a standard report for this.

However, I can suggest a workaround that might help:

  1. Go to 'Define Production Scheduling Profile for Process order' or 'Define Production Scheduling Profile for Production order'.
  2. Set the automatic batch creation to 'Automatic Batch Creation at Time of Order Release' for the respective production scheduling profile.
  3. Navigate to 'Mass Processing Production Orders' app or 'Mass Processing Process Orders' app.
  4. Mass release the Process/Production orders.

Based on this configuration, the batches will be created during the orders release.

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Enterprise Architecture and how to get started
entry.content:
Great article Taryn.

Being on the road for almost the same time than you and currently leading the IT Strategy for a retail company, I would like to endorse your words.

I strongly agree with the emphasis on selecting architecture artifacts based on the needs of stakeholders rather than adhering strictly to architectural frameworks for their own sake. In my experience, aligning architecture work products—such as principles, standards, and guidelines—with the specific business requirements and objectives is critical for ensuring relevance and buy-in from both business and IT stakeholders. This approach also fosters collaboration and reduces friction when it comes to implementing changes or improvements.

Furthermore, I appreciate the acknowledgment that architecture projects are rarely linear. The iterative nature of the process is essential for adapting to evolving business needs and technology landscapes, and this flexibility helps ensure that we can adjust to new insights, whether they come from user feedback, evolving market conditions, or new technologies.

Much have changed in the last 30 years. But much stays the same, like these principles.

entry.title: What’s New in Job Monitoring for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
What’s New in Job Monitoring for SAP Cloud ALM

SAP Cloud ALM continues to evolve as an indispensable tool for managing and monitoring SAP landscapes. The latest updates to Job Monitoring bring significant enhancements, enabling organizations to efficiently manage automated processes, improve transparency, and streamline operations.

This blog explores these updates in depth, offering insights into how they can empower businesses to optimize their IT operations and adapt to evolving business needs.

Introduction to Job Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM

In SAP environments, managing scheduled jobs and background tasks across multiple systems can be challenging. SAP Cloud ALM's Job Monitoring simplifies this by providing a centralized view of all scheduled processes, alerts for failures or delays, and actionable insights to improve operational efficiency.

The recent updates build on this foundation with advanced features such as granular alerting, enhanced notifications, new job types for monitoring, and an intuitive user interface. Let’s dive into the details.

Key Features and Enhancements

1. Comprehensive Notifications for Better Insights

Keeping stakeholders informed is critical. The enhanced notification system in SAP Cloud ALM now provides detailed insights into job execution:

  • Run Information: Notifications include the start time, end time, and a unique Run ID.
  • Metrics: Execution status and application status are displayed to identify issues quickly.
  • Execution Details: Information on the executable and who executed it is included for better traceability.

Chat notifications have also been improved, enabling users to access job metrics directly within chat tools for faster collaboration.

2. Advanced Monitoring Options

Monitor Identity Provisioning Jobs

With the latest updates, you can now monitor the job execution data of SAP Cloud Identity Services, specifically the Identity Provisioning service type. This allows better tracking and management of identity-related tasks across your organization.

Support for SAP Business Workflow Monitoring

Job Monitoring now includes SAP Business Workflow jobs, broadening the scope of monitored job types.

Job Type-Specific Monitoring

Administrators can monitor jobs based on the type, such as:

  • SAP Application Jobs
  • SAP ABAP Jobs
  • SAP Business Workflow Jobs

To focus on specific processes, data collection can be enabled or disabled for individual job types within managed components.

3. Flexible Alerting and Event Management

SAP Cloud ALM introduces a series of enhancements to alerting and event handling, enabling users to respond proactively to issues:

Customizable Alerts for Execution Status

Alerts can now be restricted to specific execution statuses, such as:

  • Red (critical): Indicating severe issues.
  • Yellow (warning): Highlighting potential problems.

Users can configure alerts based on their operational priorities, minimizing unnecessary notifications.

New Alert Types

Two new alert types have been added:

  • Critical Runtime: Raised when a job exceeds the expected runtime.
  • Critical Delay: Triggered for significant delays in job execution.

Event Filtering by Job Scheduler

A new "Scheduled By" filter allows events to be filtered based on the user who scheduled the job, adding granularity to monitoring.

4. Redesigned User Interface for Simplified Navigation

Home Page Redesign

The Overview Page has been renamed to Home and revamped to display:

  • Job metrics and insights tailored to the selected scope and time frame.
  • Alerts and automated actions grouped by service or system.

This redesign improves usability and provides a clearer overview of monitored processes.

Improved Scope Selector

The updated scope selector offers a more intuitive interface, making it easier for users to define and switch between scopes.

5. Enhanced Analytics and Reporting

New Analysis Page

A tabular Analysis Page offers deeper insights into job performance, including:

  • Average Runtime: Helps identify performance trends.
  • Failure Rates: Pinpoints problem areas.
  • Total Executions: Highlights job volumes for a given period.

Grouped Job View

Jobs are now grouped in the Monitoring Page, making it easier to analyze related jobs collectively. Grouped jobs are marked for easy identification.

6. Streamlined Configuration and Management

Global Settings Management

Administrators can:

  • View housekeeping and aggregation jobs.
  • Configure the retention time for aggregated executions based on business requirements.

Removing Configured Services or Systems

The Remove Configuration feature allows administrators to delete all configurations for a service or system, ensuring a clean setup.

7. Event Handling and Automation

The latest updates enable greater automation and flexibility in event handling:

  • Triggering Event Actions: Configure event actions such as email notifications, chat messages, or automated workflows for specific job events.
  • Action on Every Occurrence: Raise events (e.g., emails, alerts) for every red or yellow status occurrence.

Precision Timing for Event Actions

Previously limited to minutes, event actions can now be configured in seconds, minutes, or hours, providing more control over event response times.

8. Calendar View for Service Events

The Home Page now includes a calendar icon on each card, enabling users to view:

  • Ongoing events for services and systems.
  • Scheduled events within the next hour.

Practical Use Cases

Scenario 1: Ensuring Timely Identity Provisioning

An organization can monitor SAP Cloud Identity Services jobs to ensure that identity-related tasks (e.g., user creation) are executed without delay. Alerts for critical delays or failures ensure immediate corrective action.

Scenario 2: Managing Large Job Volumes

Using the new analysis tools, administrators can identify trends in job runtime and failure rates, optimizing system performance by addressing bottlenecks.

Scenario 3: Enhancing SLA Adherence

By configuring alerts for critical runtimes or execution delays, businesses can improve adherence to service-level agreements (SLAs).

Key Benefits of These Updates

  • Enhanced Efficiency: Automate repetitive monitoring tasks and focus on exceptions.
  • Improved Collaboration: Notifications and alerts provide actionable insights to all stakeholders.
  • Proactive Issue Management: Address potential failures before they escalate.
  • Scalability: Monitor a variety of job types and integrate them seamlessly across services.

Conclusion

The latest updates to Job Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM demonstrate SAP's commitment to innovation and operational excellence. With detailed analytics, flexible alerting, and streamlined user interfaces, SAP Cloud ALM empowers businesses to optimize their IT landscapes and achieve better outcomes.

Explore these features today to see how they can transform your operations. For more information, visit the SAP Help Portal.

 

Keep learning and keep sharing.

Cheers!

entry.title: Configuring Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) In SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)
entry.content:
Have you ever wondered how to predict the Probability Of Failure (PoF) for a technical object associated with a specific failure mode based on its age?. In this blog, I would like to highlight the essential features of Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) in SAP Asset Performance Management (APM).

Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) is a feature within APM that allows the maintenance team to predict when a technical object could fail and how likely  failure would occur given the age of the technical object. The graph that FCA produces is based on Weibull model, a commonly used data model within the realm of reliability. System uses malfunction notifications to train and score the FCA model and visualize the failure data. On the graph, we can view upper and lower confidence intervals (the range in values of likely failure given the technical object`s age) and Probability Of Failure (PoF).

SAP Asset Performance Management

My Business Use Case:

We run an air compressor with a standby equipment continuously to operate the kiln girth gear lubrication system. As a maintenance engineer, I would like to predict the Probability Of Failure (PoF) using Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) in SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) for one of the air compressors before the planned outage in Jul 2025.

My Technical Object Structure in S/4 HANA Asset Management:

MTTR/MTBR Analysis for the technical object 10001729 in S/4 HANA Asset Management:

Before we dive into Failure Curve Analytics, I would also like to touch base on other alternatives to predict next failure date for the technical object 10001729 using MTBF(Mean Time Between Failure) average values. 

  • I assume failure occurs at regular intervals and the next failure date is simply:

  • I assume failure occurs randomly over time and use random model with exponential distribution to predict next failure date:

 

Now, let us dive into Failure Curve Analytics and see when the Air Compressor will fail if it runs continuously.

First things first, check the integrity of the Air Compressor 10001729 in SAP APM.

To view the actual failure curve graph, we need to create a FCA model. FCA model needs notifications to train and score. For now, all the notifications & failure modes that will be used to train and score have already been created in the S/4 HANA Asset Management system that is associated with the SAP APM system. 

Create a Technical Object Group and assign the respective technical objects which are being used for FCA model.

 

 

Creating Failure Curve Analytics Model:

 

If we have any model configurations, we can see them once we click on "Configure Failure Curve Analytics".

I have created a new FCA model as,

  • The age for conditional probability of failure is used to calculate the conditional probability of the failure. For example, if you enter 100 days as the age and your technical object has a current age of 50 days, then the conditional probability of failure is calculated for the age of 100 days given that the technical object has not failed until the age of 50 days. If you leave blank, then system calculates only the probability of failure.
  • First Installation date is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its first failure. If installation date is not maintained, the first failure date is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its next failure.
  • If technical object is repairable and restored to a "like-new" condition and set to the age of 0 once it is repaired. This age is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its next failure. If not repairable, the installation date is used to calculate the age of the technical object at its failure.

Under Input Data Sets, let us assign the technical objects that are being trained and scored within the FCA model. We can "Add" or "Remove" the technical objects from the model.

Now, let us assign the failure modes. All the failure modes associated with the failure data profile of the technical object used in FCA model are displayed. We can check off the failure modes to be excluded from training and scoring of the model.

Note: It is considered to have no more than 40 failure modes associated with the model.

Now let us specify the malfunction notification date range to train and score the FCA model. A default date range is provided by the system, although this can be altered if desired.

 

Training and Scoring the Model:

With all the input Data Sets properly configured, the FCA model AC_SL200_FCA can now be trained and scored.

If the training and scoring fails, system displays the failure list that prevented the model from completing successful training and scoring and the status will be set to "Failed".

If the training and scoring of the model are both successful, the status of the both will be set to "Completed".

 

If we want to run the model at a different interval, we can still change the interval to DAY, WEEK & MONTH.

 

Viewing the Analytics Graph of the FCA Model:

Now the FCA Model "AC_SL200_FCA" has been successfully trained and scored. The FCA analytical graph can be viewed within the technical object details page. 

Let us navigate to "Explore Technical Objects" tile.

 

on the graph, there are three curves. The main one is probability of failure curve, which is the likely percent chance the equipment 10001729 will fail because of the failure mode "Low Discharge Pressure" selected at a specified age in days. The upper and lower confidence intervals mark the highest and lowest percent chance that the equipment 10001729 will fail due to the "Low Discharge Pressure" failure mode. The predicted failure date is Jan 8, 2025, and the time to failure is  47 days.

Key Note:

The MTBF approach to predict next failure date based on average values can mask variations and specific trends in the failure data. They assume constant failure rate, and might not reflect reality.

The FCA with Weibull distribution is more flexible and can represent various failure modes and can model different failure behaviors over time. When we consider systems and their components, they often do not fail at constant rate, So the FCA analysis can account for varying failure rates over time and fits the actual failure data to a curve and predicts when future failures might occur, where as the MTBF cannot. 

This brings me to the end of my process walk-through. I hope this blog proves useful to Plant Maintenance team. I look forward to seeing Plant Maintenance team using SAP APM Failure Curve Analytics to predict the technical object failure. 

If you would like to keep up on SAP Sustainability Control Tower, Asset Management and Asset Performance Management, follow me on SAP Community and LinkedIn.

 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

My LinkedIn Profile: www.linkedin.com/in/pardhreddyc

entry.title: QA08 Activate despite of Stock is not creating NEW inspection lot for QI stock without lots
entry.content:
Hi Everybody,

We are moving material from Plant A of Company C1 to Plant B of Company C2 via Purchase Order.

1. When the material is received at Plant B, there should be no Inspection Lot, and the material should go to UNRESTRICTED

2. This should happen only if the supplier is Plant A

3. In case of any other vendor, the system should create Inspection Lot and move the stock to QI stock

We have made the following settings:

1. Inspection Type 01 is active for the material

2. Post to insp stock check box is checked for Inspection Type 01

3. QM proc active check box is not checked in QM view of Material Master

4. Q-Info record is maintained for material, Plant A and Plant B combination

5. In the Q-Info record Insp. controls has the value 'No Inspection'

6. GR is with reference to Inbound Delivery in VL32N.

7. We have Packing and Handling Units linked

The requirement is as follows.

1. Before implementing the above said scenario for some reason stock went to QI without lots and we need this to get transferred into unrestricted or to create valid inspection lot.

2. I expected QA08 transaction can help us in create inspection lots by using 'Activate despite of stock' option with inspection type '01', '08' or '0800'.

3. But this didn't create NEW lot for QI stocks without lots.

Is QA08 really has this functionality?

Since we are working with Inbound delivery and packing with HU, reverse posting of document is hectic.

Any work around?

 

Thanks, With Regards,

Dinesh Kumar.
