entry.title: Re: European Sustainability Reporting Standards(ESRS) Journey With SAP Sustainability Control Tower(
entry.content:
Hi Leticia,

Thank you! I appreciate your kind words.

1) This question is about SCT data collection in general, not specifically to ESRS. I have read that for the measure amount you can import data from S/4 HANA. However, you can only do it once, and then the task would be marked as complete, right?  How would you handle an scenario with multiple ERPs, for example, for a complex multinational? Is there a way to import from multiple ERPs?

=> Yes, once the data is imported without errors, the import status becomes "published". If a customer has multiple ERP systems, and does not want to go with the file upload method, I would recommend using either SAP Datasphere or Inbound APIs. For GHG emissions, If the customer has SAP EHSM-Environment Management, the CO2e calculation for each business location can be done in EHSM-EM, though the customer maintains multiple ERPS. Once the calculation is done in EHSM-EM, customer can import the emissions data using the option "Import From SAP EHS Management".

2) About ESRS, do the metrics include the ESRS EFRAG Code? Is it possible to include it in a report?

=> If I understand correctly, you are asking how to get the ESRS E1,E2,E3 etc., codes for disclosure requirement in ESRS report that is being generated from SAP SCT? - If so, the report has already got the disclosure requirement codes.

3) You included the following comment in your blog "Please bear in mind that the transition of ESRS journey with SAP SCT is underway".  What does this mean exactly and when would the transition be completed?

=> I mean, SAP is working to fully align the SCT metrics with the disclosure requirements of the ESRS definitions. We need to check the SCT Road Map Explorer to get more insights on it. Expecting more content in Q1 2025.

4) Are you familiar with the ESRS Disclosure Management Content from PAPM (ESRS Disclosure Management | SAP Help Portal)?. Have you explored it?

=> Yes, I explored. It`s a different cloud solution and covers all 12 standards of ESRS disclosure requirements. Right now, I am focusing mainly on SAP SCT to achieve ESRS & EU taxonomy reporting.

--

Regards,

Pardha Reddy.C

entry.title: Re: SAP Data retrieval inconsistency
entry.content: SAP doesn't offer any official way to access tables generically. Solutions: access the database directly (custom solution) instead of going to it indirectly through the ABAP system or develop a custom RFC-enabled function module. For more information about RFC_READ_TABLE, see the note https://me.sap.com/notes/382318.

entry.title: Mmoexp POE currency: Traps and Hazards Are Unpredictable
entry.content:
Extended Runs:
The Uber Labyrinth is a lengthy run, and sometimes you’ll want to take breaks, especially during high-stress sections. If you feel the need to stop, reset, or take a breather, a Portal Scroll will let you safely exit the Labyrinth. Moreover, it gives you a chance to POE currency adjust your gear, change skills, or manage your flasks and other resources.

Traps and Hazards Are Unpredictable:
While knowing the layout of the Uber Labyrinth is useful, traps and enemies can still surprise you, especially in certain layouts with unexpected traps or particularly tough monster configurations. If you’re caught off guard, Portal Scrolls give you a chance to retreat, reassess, and come back prepared.

Multiple Stages:
The Uber Labyrinth has several stages, each with its own challenges, and there are no checkpoints between these stages. If you die in one of these stages or run out of POE currency buy resources, you can use a Portal Scroll to go back to town, refuel, and come back fresh. Without this option, you risk losing all the progress you’ve made.

How Many Portal Scrolls Should You Bring?
There is no one-size-fits-all answer to how many Portal Scrolls you should bring, but here are some factors to consider when planning:

entry.title: Mmoexp CFB 25 Coins: Thriving Beyond Football
entry.content:
Embrace the Unknown: Thriving Beyond Football
Transitioning from a football career to life beyond the gridiron can be an overwhelming and uncertain experience. The familiar routines, the competitive spirit, and the sense of purpose can all seem like they are fading away. However, embracing the unknown during this transition is not only necessary but can also lead to new opportunities and growth that you may never have anticipated.

3. Seek New Experiences and Embrace Curiosity
You’ve spent years focusing on football, but the world outside offers countless opportunities to learn, grow, and explore new passions. Embrace curiosity and dive into areas of interest that were once outside your radar.

Explore New Hobbies: Whether it’s starting a business, exploring art, or diving into a new field of study, let your curiosity guide you to new and exciting places. New experiences help you find joy in the unknown and allow you to build a richer, more diverse life.
Network and Learn from Others: Seek out people who have navigated similar transitions or who are experts in fields that intrigue you. Their stories and experiences can provide valuable insights and help you expand your horizons.
4. Be Adaptable and Flexible
Life after football may not always go as CFB 25 Coins planned, and the path may be filled with unexpected turns. Flexibility is key to thriving in the unknown.

Adapt Quickly: Be ready to adjust your plans as new opportunities or challenges arise. Embrace change, even if it doesn’t align with your original goals or timeline.
Reinvent Yourself: Don’t be afraid to College Football 25 Coins redefine your personal and professional identity. Your career in football doesn’t define who you are—it’s just one chapter in your life story.

entry.title: Mmoexp Elden Ring Runes: Keep an Eye Out for NPCs and Quests
entry.content:
6. Keep an Eye Out for NPCs and Quests
Throughout your travels, you’ll encounter NPCs that offer quests or Elden Ring Runes assistance. Remaining flexible and engaging with these NPCs can yield valuable rewards and storyline progression.

Questlines: Many NPCs have intricate questlines that take you to unique areas or reward you with special items and spells. Make sure to explore thoroughly and revisit key locations to trigger quest stages.
Helpful NPCs: Some NPCs provide helpful services like healing, buffing, or selling rare items. Be sure to engage with them regularly.
7. Experiment with Fast Travel and Sites of Grace
Fast travel is essential for efficient exploration in Elden Ring. Use Sites of Grace as checkpoints and travel points.

Optimal Sites of Grace: Set up camp at key Sites of Grace to Elden Ring Runes buy ensure you have a nearby travel point when revisiting areas. This saves time when re-exploring zones or farming for specific items.
Travel Between Regions: Once you unlock the ability to fast travel, you can easily jump between regions to access new content, bosses, or special farming locations.
